body:
## Correction + verified config details for Codex and Gemini

I did a full documentation pass on both CLIs (installed versions **codex 0.142.5**, **gemini-cli 0.49.0**) and need to **correct** one claim in the issue body: the official Gemini CLI **can** be pointed at a custom base URL — I was wrong to say it can't. This actually *strengthens* the case for native Gemini support here.

### Codex — no config workaround; streaming is mandatory
From the official config reference (`developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference`), `model_providers.<id>` supports: `base_url`, `env_key`, `env_http_headers`, `http_headers`, `query_params`, `request_max_retries` (4), `stream_max_retries` (5), `stream_idle_timeout_ms` (300000), `wire_api`.
- **`wire_api`: `"responses"` is the only supported value** — `chat` is removed. (Matches the runtime error we saw.)
- **There is no option to disable streaming / force non-streaming**, and no model-listing/metadata key.
- **Conclusion:** Codex *always* streams over the Responses API. The only fix is server-side Responses SSE — tracked as **#602 / sub-issue S2**. Re-tested `codex exec "hi"` on 0.142.5: fails identically (`stream disconnected before completion`).

### Gemini — base-URL override DOES exist (correction)
The gemini-cli 0.49.0 config reference documents:
- **`GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL`** — *"Overrides the default base URL for Gemini API requests (when using `gemini-api-key` authentication)."* Explicitly permits `http://localhost` / `127.0.0.1` (HTTPS requirement waived for localhost).
- **`GOOGLE_VERTEX_BASE_URL`** — same, for `vertex-ai` auth.
- **`CODE_ASSIST_ENDPOINT`** — overrides the OAuth code-assist endpoint.

**Key point:** `GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL` changes only *where* the request is sent, **not the wire protocol** — gemini-cli still sends its native `generateContent` / `streamGenerateContent` shape (not OpenAI). So the official CLI **can** be aimed at our server via:

```sh
export GEMINI_API_KEY="anything"                       # selects gemini-api-key auth
export GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL="http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/gemini"
gemini -m gemini-... -p "hi"
```

…and "hi" will work **as soon as our server answers the native Gemini `generateContent` protocol (+ its SSE) at that base URL** — i.e. **sub-issue S4**. My earlier `IneligibleTierError` was caused by leaving it on the default Google-OAuth path (which ignores the base URL); the correct path is `gemini-api-key` auth **plus** `GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL`.

**Net effect on this issue:** native Gemini support (S4) is even more valuable than first stated — it makes the *real, official Gemini CLI* work against Formal AI, not just a Gemini SDK. Same for Vertex via `GOOGLE_VERTEX_BASE_URL` (S5). This aligns cleanly with the proposed `/api/gemini/...` and `/api/vertex/...` prefixes.

## Added ask: clear per-protocol / per-CLI configuration documentation

This umbrella should also deliver **clear, copy-pasteable documentation** for configuring each supported client CLI/SDK against Formal AI, verified to print a `hi` reply. At minimum:

- **Codex** — `model_providers` block, `wire_api = "responses"`, `codex exec "hi"`. (see #602)
- **opencode** — `opencode.json` custom `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` provider, `opencode run -m <provider>/<model> "hi"`. (see #604)
- **Gemini CLI** — `GEMINI_API_KEY=<any>` + `GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/gemini` (`gemini-api-key` auth), `gemini -p "hi"`. (sub-issue S4)
- **Vertex** — `GOOGLE_VERTEX_BASE_URL` + `vertex-ai` auth. (sub-issue S5)
- Base URLs per protocol prefix (`/api/openai/v1`, `/api/gemini/...`, `/api/vertex/...`, `/api/anthropic/...`).

**Acceptance addition (S7):** each documented client config is verified end-to-end (`hi` returns a reply) and the doc requires no wire-format reverse-engineering.
Blocks #606 (the `with-formal-ai` wrapper CLI, which can only complete a turn once this is resolved).
Related sub-work: #607 (bash/shell tool_calls) and #608 (standard reasoning/thinking fields + streaming) — both feed the multi-protocol client-compatibility goal.
